name: Malpica - return via Alto de Fruxil
name_original: Malpica - vuelta por Alto de Fruxil
description: This moderate 23.8-mile (38.3 km) road cycling route from Malpica features coastal views and a climb to Alto de Fruxil.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Malpica De Bergantiños traverse a dramatic coastline characterized by rugged cliffs and the powerful Atlantic Ocean. The region offers a blend of coastal and inland landscapes, featuring pristine beaches and diverse terrain. Cyclists can expect well-maintained routes that cater to various fitness levels, with varying elevation gains. The area's physical features provide a picturesque backdrop for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Malpica De Bergantiños The most popular road cycling route is Malpica - return via Alto de Fruxil, a 23.8 miles (38.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and includes a notable climb. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Beo Beach – Carballo loop from O Escorial, a moderate 38.2 miles (61.5 km) path. This route provides extensive coastal scenery and inland sections. Local road cyclists also love the Carballo – Playa de Area Maior loop from A Area de Canido, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas and towards Area Maior beach, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Road cycling around Malpica De Bergantiños is defined by its rugged coastline, dramatic cliffs, and scenic inland landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to those requiring very good fitness. The routes in Malpica De Bergantiños are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Malpica De Bergantiños's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Malpica De Bergantiños, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. The komoot community has explored these routes over 600 times, providing valuable insights.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: The routes in Malpica De Bergantiños cater to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix, with 13 moderate routes and 7 more challenging options. There is also 1 easy route available for those looking for a gentler ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: Road cycling in Malpica De Bergantiños features a dramatic coastline with rugged cliffs and the powerful Atlantic Ocean, alongside scenic inland landscapes. Routes are generally well-maintained, offering a blend of coastal views and varied terrain, including some notable climbs.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Beo Beach – Carballo loop from O Escorial&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain, with over 760 meters of ascent over its 61.5 km distance. This route is ideal for cyclists with very good fitness.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can expect spectacular panoramic views of the rugged coastline and the Atlantic Ocean. Notable features include pristine beaches like Beo and Area Maior, and the unique Sisargas Islands visible from the coast. Don't miss the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/397238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Nariga Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roncudo Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, which offer stunning vistas.

Q: Are there specific viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3619962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sisargas Islands&lt;/a&gt; from Mirador de San Adrián is particularly renowned for its suggestive panoramic views of the islands and the coastline, especially beautiful at sunset. Many coastal routes will also offer numerous scenic overlooks.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Beo Beach – Carballo loop from O Escorial&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38276279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carballo – Playa de Area Maior loop from A Area de Canido&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular choices among local cyclists.

Q: What is a popular moderate road cycling route in the area?
A: A popular moderate route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974605133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpica - return via Alto de Fruxil&lt;/a&gt;. This 38.3 km (23.8 miles) trail offers beautiful coastal views and includes a notable climb, making it a rewarding ride for those with good fitness.

Q: Can I find longer road cycling routes that explore more of the region?
A: Yes, for longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laxe Beach – View of Beo Beach loop from A Area de Canido&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 55 km and features significant elevation changes, allowing you to explore more of the diverse coastal and inland landscapes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Malpica De Bergantiños?
A: The road cycling routes in Malpica De Bergantiños are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the stunning coastal scenery, the dramatic cliffs, and the variety of routes that cater to different fitness levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible by road bike in the Malpica De Bergantiños area?
A: Yes, while cycling, you can pass by or make a short detour to historical sites. The region is home to the ancient Dolmen of Pedra da Arca, dating back to the 4th and 3rd centuries BC, showcasing its megalithic culture. The 16th-century hermitage at Mirador de San Adrián also offers a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the famous lighthouses of Costa da Morte?
A: Many routes in the region offer views of or pass near the iconic lighthouses. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/397238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Nariga Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roncudo Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; are significant landmarks that provide impressive views and are often incorporated into longer rides, allowing you to experience the dramatic coastal scenery they illuminate.
tips: Malpica de Bergantiños is a small fishing village with a beautiful white sand beach. A great place to see the sunset in Punta Nariga

faqs.question: Is the Malpica - return via Alto de Fruxil route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as a moderate road ride. While it's not overly technical, the 500 meters of elevation gain, including the climb to Alto de Fruxil, means it requires a reasonable level of fitness. Beginners with some cycling experience and good stamina might find it challenging but rewarding.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for the route aren't detailed, Malpica de Bergantiños is the likely starting point. You can usually find parking in coastal towns like Malpica, especially near the harbor or beaches, which are also highlights along the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Malpica - return via Alto de Fruxil route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it generally smooth for road bikes. However, as a moderate route with significant elevation changes, expect climbs and descents, particularly around Alto de Fruxil. Some segments might occasionally be less smooth, typical for routes in this region.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers a blend of coastal and elevated landscapes. You can expect picturesque views of the sea and beaches, especially near Malpica de Bergantiños Harbor and Beach and Playa de Area Maior. The ascent to Alto de Fruxil will likely provide panoramic views of the surrounding area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Malpica area, including this route, is generally during the spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is typically milder and more stable, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the heavier rains of winter. This allows for more comfortable riding conditions and better visibility of the coastal and elevated scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: The route is located within the ZEPA Costa da Morte (Norte), which is a Special Protection Area for Birds. Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling on public roads within this area. However, always respect local regulations regarding protected natural spaces and wildlife.

faqs.question: Is the Malpica - return via Alto de Fruxil route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it's generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists directly on the road due to safety concerns. If you plan to visit the area with a dog, it's best to explore other walking trails or designated dog-friendly areas off the main cycling path. Always keep dogs on a leash in public areas and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. Approximately 2% of the route overlaps with O Camiño dos Faros. Etapa 1 Malpica-Niñóns, and about 1% runs along the beira do rio (riverside path).

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given it's a moderate road ride with elevation, you should bring your road bike in good condition, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, and some snacks. A basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers) is also recommended. Don't forget sunscreen and sunglasses, especially for coastal sections.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends near Malpica de Bergantiños, a coastal town where you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel before or after your ride. Along the route itself, especially in more rural sections or during the climb to Alto de Fruxil, options may be limited, so it's wise to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is designed as a circular or loop trail. The name "Malpica - return via Alto de Fruxil" indicates that it starts and ends in the same general area, taking a specific path to return, making it convenient for cyclists.
